basic_details.job_title: Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course 2026 – SSC (JAG) Notification for Law Graduates
basic_details.job_location: Officers Training Academy, Chennai (training); SSB at Prayagraj, Bhopal, Bengaluru, Jalandhar
basic_details.short_information: The Indian Army has invited online applications from unmarried male and female law graduates for the Short Service Commission (JAG) Course under the JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course (April 2027). This recruitment offers 10 combined vacancies in the Judge Advocate General (JAG) Branch of the Indian Army. Eligible candidates must hold an LLB degree and a valid CLAT PG 2026 score to apply. The online application process for Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course recruitment 2026 is open from 17 July 2026 to 17 August 2026 on www.joinindianarmy.nic.in.

age_limit.details: The date of birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate (or equivalent) on the date of application submission will be treated as final; no subsequent request for change will be considered.

qualification.details: Minimum 55% aggregate marks in LLB Degree (three years professional course after graduation, or five years after 10+2 examination). CLAT PG 2026 Score (conducted in December 2025) is mandatory for all candidates, including LLM-qualified and LLM-appearing candidates. No other CLAT PG exam score will be accepted. Candidates must be eligible for registration as an advocate with the Bar Council of India/State. The LLB degree must be from a College/University recognized by the Bar Council of India. All candidates applying for JAG Entry must get their CLAT PG 2026 result verified at the Selection Centre before the SSB is conducted - failure to do so makes the candidate ineligible for SSB, with no representation accepted. | Candidates holding a law degree can also explore more LLB Government Jobs and Law Graduate Government Jobs available across India.

salary_details.details: Stipend during training at OTA, Chennai: ₹56,100 per month. Rank after training: Lieutenant. Pay Level on commissioning: Level 10, ₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500. Military Service Pay (MSP): ₹15,500 per month (Lieutenant to Brigadier). Approximate CTC on commissioning: ₹17–18 lakh per annum. Additional allowances (as applicable and subject to eligibility conditions): Dearness Allowance, Para Allowance, Field Area Allowances, Dress Allowance, Transport Allowance, Children Education Allowance, Hostel Subsidy, among others specified in the notification.

selection_process: Application submission and shortlisting (based on CLAT PG 2026 score and vacancy-to-call-up ratio). Selection of SSB Centre and Date (first-come-first-served
once selected cannot be changed by the candidate). SSB Interview (two-stage selection at Prayagraj

or Jalandhar) - duration up to five days. Candidates failing Stage-I are returned the same day. Medical Examination for candidates recommended after Stage-II. Merit List preparation based on SSB marks. Joining Letter for Pre-Commission Training at OTA
Qualifying the SSB interview alone does not confirm final selection - final selection depends on the merit list and availability of vacancies.
how_to_apply: Visit www.joinindianarmy.nic.in and click on 'Officer Entry Application/Login'.
Click 'Registration' (not required if already registered on the website).
Fill the online registration form after reading the instructions carefully.
After registration, click 'Apply Online' under the Dashboard.
On the 'Officer Selection – Eligibility' page, click 'Apply' against Short Service Commission JAG Entry Course.
Fill in personal information, communication details, education details, and previous SSB details (if any) under the 'Application Form' page.
Review the summary of information, then click 'Submit' only after confirming all details are correct.
Take two printouts of the application (with roll number) 30 minutes after the online application closes on the last day.
important_instructions: No changes can be made to the online application after it closes; no representation on this will be entertained. CGPA/Grades must be converted to marks as per the university's formula before filling in the application. The candidate's name, parents' names, and date of birth in the profile and application must match the Matriculation/Secondary School certificate exactly. Only one online application per candidate is allowed; multiple applications will lead to cancellation of candidature. Candidates serving in the Armed Forces must inform their Commanding Officer in writing and carry a countersigned application to the SSB. Requests to change the SSB interview date or centre will not be entertained. Candidates must report within 7 days of their candidature reporting date, or their candidature will be cancelled. Candidates who marry after the date of application, or during training, will lose eligibility or be withdrawn from training. In case of any discrepancy between the English and Hindi versions of the notification, the English version shall prevail.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course: Quick Overview

rows.details: Officers Training Academy, Chennai (training); SSB at Prayagraj, Bhopal, Bengaluru, Jalandhar

content_sections.heading: Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course Important Dates

rows.event: Shortlisting for SSB (based on CLAT PG score)

content_sections.heading: Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: The Indian Army notifies combined vacancies for men and women based on the operational and administrative requirements of the Force, and the final selection and merit depend on the notified vacancies for this particular course.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria for Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course
content_sections.text: A citizen of India, or A subject of Nepal, or A person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Myanmar, Sri Lanka, and the East African countries of Kenya, Uganda, United Republic of Tanzania, Zambia, Malawi, Zaire, Ethiopia, and Vietnam with the intention of permanently settling in India (a certificate of eligibility from the Government of India is required for these categories, except Gorkha subjects of Nepal). Open only to unmarried male and unmarried female law graduates.

content_sections.heading: Educational Qualification
content_sections.text: Minimum 55% aggregate marks in LLB Degree (three years professional course after graduation, or five years after 10+2 examination). CLAT PG 2026 Score (conducted in December 2025) is mandatory for all candidates, including LLM-qualified and LLM-appearing candidates. No other CLAT PG exam score will be accepted. Candidates must be eligible for registration as an advocate with the Bar Council of India/State. The LLB degree must be from a College/University recognized by the Bar Council of India. All candidates applying for JAG Entry must get their CLAT PG 2026 result verified at the Selection Centre before the SSB is conducted - failure to do so makes the candidate ineligible for SSB, with no representation accepted. Candidates holding a law degree can also explore more LLB Government Jobs and Law Graduate Government Jobs available across India.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course Age Limit
content_sections.text: The date of birth recorded in the Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate (or equivalent) on the date of application submission will be treated as final; no subsequent request for change will be considered.

rows.details: Not earlier than 02 Jan 2000 and not later than 01 Jan 2006 (both dates inclusive)

content_sections.heading: Indian Army JAG Salary and Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Stipend during training at OTA, Chennai: ₹56,100 per month. Rank after training: Lieutenant. Pay Level on commissioning: Level 10, ₹56,100 – ₹1,77,500. Military Service Pay (MSP): ₹15,500 per month (Lieutenant to Brigadier). Approximate CTC on commissioning: ₹17–18 lakh per annum. Additional allowances (as applicable and subject to eligibility conditions): Dearness Allowance, Para Allowance, Field Area Allowances, Dress Allowance, Transport Allowance, Children Education Allowance, Hostel Subsidy, among others specified in the notification.

content_sections.heading: Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course Selection Process
content_sections.text: Application submission and shortlisting (based on CLAT PG 2026 score and vacancy-to-call-up ratio). Selection of SSB Centre and Date (first-come-first-served, once selected cannot be changed by the candidate). SSB Interview (two-stage selection at Prayagraj, Bhopal, Bengaluru, or Jalandhar) - duration up to five days. Candidates failing Stage-I are returned the same day. Medical Examination for candidates recommended after Stage-II. Merit List preparation based on SSB marks. Joining Letter for Pre-Commission Training at OTA, Chennai, subject to eligibility and vacancy. Qualifying the SSB interview alone does not confirm final selection - final selection depends on the merit list and availability of vacancies.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for SSB Interview
content_sections.text: Signed printout of the online application with self-attested photograph. Matriculation/Secondary School Examination Certificate (or equivalent) and mark sheet. 12th Class Certificate and Mark Sheet. Graduation Degree/Provisional Degree (for three-year LLB after graduation). LLB Degree/Provisional Degree from a recognized university. Mark sheets of all years/semesters. Registration with Bar Council of India/State, or a certificate confirming the LLB course is recognized by the Bar Council of India. University certificate on the CGPA-to-percentage conversion formula (if applicable). CLAT PG 2026 Score Card (conducted in December 2025). DigiLocker certificates will not be accepted in place of original certificates. All original documents must be carried for verification at the SSB; originals are returned after verification.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course
content_sections.text: Visit www.joinindianarmy.nic.in and click on 'Officer Entry Application/Login'. Click 'Registration' (not required if already registered on the website). Fill the online registration form after reading the instructions carefully. After registration, click 'Apply Online' under the Dashboard. On the 'Officer Selection – Eligibility' page, click 'Apply' against Short Service Commission JAG Entry Course. Fill in personal information, communication details, education details, and previous SSB details (if any) under the 'Application Form' page. Review the summary of information, then click 'Submit' only after confirming all details are correct. Take two printouts of the application (with roll number) 30 minutes after the online application closes on the last day.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: No changes can be made to the online application after it closes; no representation on this will be entertained. CGPA/Grades must be converted to marks as per the university's formula before filling in the application. The candidate's name, parents' names, and date of birth in the profile and application must match the Matriculation/Secondary School certificate exactly. Only one online application per candidate is allowed; multiple applications will lead to cancellation of candidature. Candidates serving in the Armed Forces must inform their Commanding Officer in writing and carry a countersigned application to the SSB. Requests to change the SSB interview date or centre will not be entertained. Candidates must report within 7 days of their candidature reporting date, or their candidature will be cancelled. Candidates who marry after the date of application, or during training, will lose eligibility or be withdrawn from training. In case of any discrepancy between the English and Hindi versions of the notification, the English version shall prevail.

faq.question: Q1. What is the Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course?
faq.answer: It is a recruitment scheme for granting Short Service Commission to unmarried male and female law graduates in the Judge Advocate General (JAG) Branch of the Indian Army, with training commencing in April 2027.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are there in JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course?
faq.answer: There are 10 combined vacancies for men and women.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 17 August 2026 at 1500 hrs.

faq.question: Q4. Is CLAT PG score mandatory for Indian Army JAG entry?
faq.answer: Yes. CLAT PG 2026 Score (conducted in December 2025) is mandatory for all candidates, including LLM-qualified and LLM-appearing candidates. No other CLAT PG exam score is accepted.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for Indian Army JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course?
faq.answer: Candidates must be between 21 and 27 years of age as on 1 January 2027 (born between 2 January 2000 and 1 January 2006, inclusive).

faq.question: Q6. What is the salary during training and after commissioning in JAG Branch?
faq.answer: Candidates receive a stipend of ₹56,100 per month during training at OTA, Chennai. After commissioning as a Lieutenant, the pay is at Level 10 (₹56,100–₹1,77,500) plus Military Service Pay of ₹15,500 per month, with an approximate CTC of ₹17–18 lakh per annum.

faq.question: Q7. Where will the SSB interview be conducted for JAG Entry Scheme 125th Course?
faq.answer: SSB interviews will be conducted at Selection Centres in Prayagraj (Uttar Pradesh), Bhopal (Madhya Pradesh), Bengaluru (Karnataka), and Jalandhar (Punjab).

faq.question: Q8. Can married candidates apply for the JAG Entry Scheme?
faq.answer: No, the scheme is open only to unmarried male and female law graduates. Candidates must remain unmarried until they complete training at OTA.
